questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.This makes readers <u>contemplate</u> the motives people have for performing acts of charity.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Cheerful
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the sentence.He tried to <u>improve</u> the working conditions in his office.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Merit
Fill in the blank with the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.Gentlemen always go for a <u>lawful</u> approach, but rouges choose ______ ways to solve problems.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Important
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Resourceful
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.There is also an increase in <u>juvenile</u> delinquency.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Alluring
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Agile
The balloon started to deflate after being pricked with a needle. What is the ANTONYM of ‘deflate’?

Read the given sentence carefully. Change the meaning of the sentence by replacing the underlined word with its ANTONYM from the following options.I have keen interest in reading the mind of those students who are <u>boisterous</u> at home but on their best behaviour at school.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the sentence.He purchased very <u>cheap</u> dresses.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The growth of the cell wall is very rarely <u>uniform</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The performance of the team in the lost match was <u>dismal</u>.̲
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Noble
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Vehement
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Benevolent
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.There is no <u>transparency</u> in the procedure followed by the company.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The news gave him inexpressible <u>ecstasy</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Innocent
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Cramped
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Transient
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM for the underlined word in the given sentence.He feels that wealthy people view him with <u>contempt</u> because he is poor.
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The vendor said that the apples were of <u>superior</u> quality.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Essays must be <u>concise</u> .
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.No one trusts her as her reasons are always <u>explicit</u> .
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank.Her voice, soft and ________, (shrill) flowed effortlessly through the room.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Comical
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Skeptic
Which of the following sentences contains the ANTONYM of the word ‘ignore’?
questions.option_1: Avoid foods which make you sick.
questions.option_2: He did not succeed in gaining custody of his daughter.
questions.option_3: To keep a tidy kitchen, do not neglect washing your dishes.
questions.option_4: Ritika recognised Kavita in the gathering.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM for the underlined word in the given sentence.As a punishment for his <u>betrayal</u> to his country, the spy who sold secrets was sent to prison.
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.His <u>jovial</u> nature has won him many friends.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Absurd
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Lazy
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Plausible’ from the given sentence.The trend of improvement is unlikely to continue for the foreseeable future.
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word based on the context of the sentence.He gave a <u>haughty</u> consent without honouring him with a single word.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Forbidden
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Generous
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Despondency
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.His grandfather was quite <u>grumpy</u> .
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.MOROSE
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The <u>humble</u> pupil sat on the floor with respect.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.It’s very cheap to display wealth by donning <u>gaudy</u> dresses.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The CEO called for <u>compulsory</u> participation from each employee.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Logical
